title: Git fundamentals — repository, commit, branch, merge
verdict: PASS with 1 soft finding. The reading is well-structured, faithfully traces to the corpus, stays within scope (repository/commit/branch/merge only), and meets the standard depth profile (1312 words, 6.56 min at 200 wpm). All six fixed sections are present in order, no frontmatter, sidecar parses. Primary resource [1] (git-scm.com) cited six times. No forward references. All ten sampled assertions trace to the corpus. No assessment references. One soft defect: the inline diagram reference at line 83 uses a broken relative path `../artifacts/diagram.png`; since reading.md lives inside `artifacts/`, the correct relative path is `diagram.png`. This does not affect text quality or scope but will break image rendering. Gold examples on record (2-1, 3-4 slots) are for different topics; written standards were the operative bar — reading meets them.

format_discipline.notes: All six fixed sections present in correct order. No frontmatter. Sidecar exists and parses with all required fields.

length.notes: Word count 1312 is within the 1200–1600 target. At the artifact-mix.json authoritative rate of 200 wpm, reading time is 6.56 min, within the 6–8 min window. At 230 wpm the reading falls to 5.7 min, just under the 6 min floor. The artifact-mix.json WPM (200) is the authoritative standard and passes. Soft observation only.

citations.notes: Primary resource [1] cited at lines 11, 28, 38, 55, 65, 79. All inline markers have matching bibliography entries; no orphans either direction.

no_forward_references.notes: GitHub/clone/push/pull named and deferred in a single sentence at line 156 ('not covered here') — this is the correct mention-and-defer pattern. Merge conflict introduced at line 92 with a one-sentence deferral. HEAD introduced within this topic's own scope. No concepts from future topics taught.

details.assertion: Repository has two parts: working directory and .git folder
Commit is a permanent snapshot of all files at a specific moment
Each commit carries unique ID, commit message, pointer to previous commit
main branch (or master on older systems)
Merge commit is a special commit with two parents
Merge when the branch goal is complete and code works

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined on first use with bold-term format. Concrete-first pattern followed throughout (analogy before abstraction for commit and branch). No hedging language. Sentences are direct. Minor observation: the commit subsection introduces three sub-items (unique ID, commit message, pointer) together in a numbered list — mitigated by list formatting, not a jargon dump.

soft_findings.detail: Diagram image reference uses path `../artifacts/diagram.png`. Since reading.md is located inside the `artifacts/` directory, the correct relative path to the sibling diagram file is `diagram.png`. The current path resolves one level above artifacts and back in, which will break image rendering in most Markdown renderers.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 4 sentences. Worked Example uses numbered steps throughout. Inline examples present within Key Concepts sections (commit message table, branch diagram). Bold-term definitions used consistently.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights appear in the reading artifact. (Note: the corpus section 7 references Assessment 4, but that text was correctly excluded from the reading.)

scope.notes: Scope held to exactly the four headline concepts: repository, commit, branch, merge. GitHub/remote operations named and deferred in one sentence. Merge conflict named and deferred in one sentence. Staging (`git add`) treated as a mechanical sub-step of commit, which is within scope and supported by the corpus Implementation section.

gold_example_match.notes: No gold example exists for topic 13.11 or its module. Operative bar was the written standards in artifact-mix.json + reading-critic SKILL.md. Reading meets the structural, scope, readability, and formatting requirements of those standards.
